label: Card
purpose: Outer container shell for card-pattern UI. Imposes layout and surface styling only — inner content is entirely free-form via <slot />. Renders as <a> when href is provided (whole card is clickable), <div> otherwise. Introduces no new CSS.

props.name: variant
props.description: Controls the CSS class(es) applied to the card shell. Each variant has a distinct layout and surface.

props.name: href
props.description: If provided, the entire card becomes an <a> element. Without href, renders as <div>. Do not put other interactive elements (buttons, inputs) inside a Card with href — that creates invalid nested interactives.

props.name: class
props.description: Appended to the generated class string after the variant class(es). Never replaces them.

props.name: ...rest
props.description: Spread onto the root element. Use for target, rel, aria-label, data-* attributes.

variants.description: Light surface (--bg-primary), hard offset shadow (--shadow-md), border (--border-primary). Lifts on hover (translateY -2px, --shadow-hover). Standard experiment card in the Playground section.

Yellow surface (--bg-accent), same shadow and hover as default. Badge tag variant inverts automatically inside accent card (.play-card--accent .play-card-tag: black bg + yellow text). Use sparingly — one accent card per group maximum.
variants.note: Applies both play-card and play-card--accent classes.

variants.description: Light surface (--bg-primary), row layout for date-block + content + arrow icon. Hard offset shadow and hover lift same as default. Distinct from default/accent — inner content pattern is different and not interchangeable.

renderRule: href provided → renders as <a> (whole card is a link). No href → renders as <div>. Element type is controlled solely by the href prop.

slotStructure.note: Card imposes no inner template. However, each variant has an established inner structure. Follow these for visual consistency.
slotStructure.default: play-card-header (Badge + optional coming-soon Badge) → div (play-card-title + play-card-desc) → span.play-card-cta
slotStructure.diary: div.diary-date-block (diary-date-day + diary-date-month) → div.diary-content (diary-title + diary-meta[Badge + diary-read-time]) → svg.diary-arrow

accessibility: Do not nest interactive elements (buttons, inputs, links) inside a Card that has href — the card is already an <a> and nested interactives are invalid HTML
When Card has href, add aria-label if the slot content alone is not descriptive enough for screen readers
The hardcoded SVG arrow stroke (#0A0A0A) in the diary slot pattern is intentional for light surfaces only — do not use the diary pattern on dark surfaces
whenToUse: default: standard experiment or project cards in the Playground section
accent: featured or highlighted experiment (use sparingly — one per group max)
diary: diary/article entry rows in the Playground diary list
whenNotToUse: diary variant for experiment content — the row layout has no space for a description block
default or accent variants for diary entries — the column layout has no date-block or arrow pattern
Nesting interactive elements (buttons, links, inputs) inside a Card with href — creates invalid nested interactives
Adding custom background, border, or radius styles to the slot content — Card owns the surface
As a non-card layout primitive — Card carries card-specific styles (shadow, hover lift, border) that are wrong for generic containers
notes: accent variant on yellow surface automatically inverts the play-card-tag Badge color (.play-card--accent .play-card-tag applies black bg + yellow text). This is automatic — no extra work needed.
The diary SVG arrow uses stroke='#0A0A0A' (hardcoded). This is intentional for the light diary card surface. Do not change without a system-level decision.
Card has no raw CSS mimics found in product code — it is the only system component with 100% import-based usage wherever it appears.

usageConstraints.reason: Card.astro cannot be imported into .jsx React islands (WorkSection.jsx, Footer.jsx, Header.jsx, TweaksPanel.jsx). React islands that need card-like structure must use raw CSS classes (play-card, diary-card) directly.
